A probe has been launched into a serious medical incident in which a six‑year‑old boy’s heart stopped for about 16 minutes at Hong Kong Children’s Hospital after his breathing tube came loose from the ventilator.
The hospital on Saturday said it was “deeply concerned” about the case.

news.headline: Tens of thousands march against far right in London ahead of local elections
news.summary: Tens of thousands of protesters marched through central London on Saturday in a major show of opposition to the far right, weeks before key elections and amid growing support for hard-right movements in the UK.

news.headline: Bank of America to pay out $72.5m over Epstein lawsuit
news.summary: The settlement comes after a lawsuit accused the bank of facilitating Epstein's sex trafficking operation

news.summary:

news.headline: France unveils aid to shield key sectors from soaring fuel prices
news.summary: Facing soaring fuel prices from the Middle East conflict, the French government has announced €70 million in targeted aid for April, helping transport, fishing, and agriculture. Measures include a 20-cent-per-litre fuel subsidy for small transport businesses and fishing boats, plus zero excise on ag

news.headline: Twenty-two migrants die off Greek coast after six days at sea
news.summary: Poor weather and a lack of food and water contributed to the deaths, the Greek coastguard says.

news.headline: Deadly Russian strikes damage port, maternity hospital
news.summary: Russian air attacks across Ukraine early Saturday killed at least four people and damaged critical infrastructure, including a port and a maternity hospital, authorities said, as Russia pressed on with its war against Ukraine. FRANCE 24's Gulliver Cragg reports from Kyiv.

news.summary: Fears of an Israeli occupation of southern Lebanon are growing among residents as more and more people become displaced due to ongoing air strikes, FRANCE 24's Olivia Bizot said, reporting from Beirut. news.headline: Cop martyred in terrorist attack on police vehicle in Bannu
news.summary:
<p>BANNU: A head constable was martyred when a police vehicle in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa’s Bannu district came under attack on Saturday, with the ensuing fire exchange killing two terrorists, according to police.</p>

news.headline: Halfway Through Lent, a Small Quebec Island Celebrates With Masks and Jigs
news.summary: Few islanders still observe Lent, but they cling to a tradition once seen as defying the all-powerful Roman Catholic Church.

news.headline: 1 month, 2 straits, more strikes: how long will the US-Israeli war on Iran last?
news.summary:
As the US-Israeli war on Iran enters its second month, another major shipping lane is at risk of closure but the chances of a prolonged conflict remain slim, according to Chinese analysts.

news.headline: Ukraine’s Zelensky agrees to air defence cooperation with UAE, Qatar on Gulf tour
news.summary:

news.headline: Morocco claims AFCON case closed, despite Senegal appeals to CAF and CAS
news.summary: Morocco believe their successful appeal against their 1-0 defeat by Senegal means the case of the AFCON crown is closed.
